headline: Microsoft’s New Windows Fix Ignores Persistent User Hostility
lede: Microsoft claims it is cleaning up Windows 11 by removing recent bloatware. Beneath the marketing, however, the systemic issues of forced telemetry and account locks remain untouched.

headline: Opera Remembers the Internet of 1996
lede: As the browser marks thirty years of development, it invites users to traverse the early web’s fragmented landscape. This interactive project serves as a digital map for an era before the Chromium monoculture.

headline: Bypassing Proprietary Intercoms with ESP32 Relays
lede: When building management abandoned the intercom system, a hardware-level override became the only logical path. By tapping into the solenoid control, we restored gate access using modern smart home protocols.

headline: Lnav Provides High-Speed Log File Navigation
lede: Stop tailing logs through complex server configurations. Lnav bundles SQLite-powered querying into a single, terminal-native application designed for immediate data extraction.

headline: ProofShot Gives AI Coding Agents Sight
lede: Debugging a layout shouldn't require manual browser inspection for every iteration. ProofShot captures the agent's work in real-time, delivering a visual audit trail directly to the developer.

headline: Why Ripgrep Outperforms Traditional Search Tools
lede: Performance isn't accidental; it is the result of architectural precision. By leveraging SIMD optimizations and efficient finite automata, ripgrep redefined the speed of codebase exploration.

headline: Autonomously Improving Legacy Research Models
lede: ECLIP experiments represent the next frontier in autonomous research. By deploying a Claude-powered agent in a closed-loop training environment, researchers successfully refined hyperparameter configurations through iterative cycles.

headline: Qite.js Returns to SSR Basics Without NPM
lede: Modern web development has become tethered to complex build chains and massive dependency trees. Qite.js offers a path forward by focusing on native modules and server-side rendering over abstractions.
pullquote: The most efficient way to build for the web is to stop building altogether

headline: BIO Co-Processor Solves FPGA Timing Latency
lede: Bao I/O minimizes the overhead of standard FPGA peripherals by employing a lean RISC-V core. This design replaces rigid instruction sets with deterministic, blocking semantics for high-performance timing.

headline: Running Massive Language Models on Mobile Hardware
lede: A smartphone running a 400 billion parameter model pushes current hardware to its theoretical edge. While token speeds remain limited, the feat proves the viability of local, private AI inference.
